title: The Futurama Holiday Spectacular

jokes.text: It's nut so good!
jokes.setup: Character eating an unshelled nut from Gunderson's
jokes.punchline: Pun on 'not so good' but with 'nut'
jokes.explanation: Simple pun for commercial parody. Very economical but predictable wordplay.

jokes.text: Something about Xmas just doesn't feel like Christmas
jokes.setup: Fry lamenting the holiday doesn't feel right
jokes.punchline: In Futurama's world, Christmas has become 'Xmas' - a violent holiday with killer robots
jokes.explanation: Good character moment establishing the world's twisted version of Christmas traditions.

jokes.text: God rest ye merry gentlemen... in peace
jokes.setup: Traditional Christmas carol being sung
jokes.punchline: Robot Santa adds 'in peace' making it sound like a death threat/eulogy
jokes.explanation: Clever subversion of familiar carol with character-appropriate menace. Very economical and effective.

jokes.text: It's the violent-est season of the year / And Krinklebot has come dispensing mugs of Xmas fear
jokes.setup: Parodying 'It's the Most Wonderful Time of the Year'
jokes.punchline: 'Violent-est' and 'mugs of fear' instead of joy
jokes.explanation: Good song parody setup establishing the violent Xmas theme with clever wordplay.

jokes.text: On Xmas Eve, we don our gay apparel / kevlar vests / asbestos stockings and a barrel
jokes.setup: Traditional 'gay apparel' from Christmas songs
jokes.punchline: List of protective gear instead of festive clothing
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalating list with perfect rhythm and absurd protective gear replacing festive wear.

jokes.text: And if grandma's Xmas fruitcake finally reaches critical mass / it can be re-gifted straight to Santa's ass
jokes.setup: Fruitcake jokes are Christmas cliche
jokes.punchline: Nuclear physics + crude insult making it a weapon
jokes.explanation: Takes tired fruitcake premise and elevates it with nuclear physics and crude humor. Very quotable.

jokes.text: The one thing that you need to make your Xmas day splendiferous / is a pine tree / a pine tree that's coniferous
jokes.setup: Building up to something important for Xmas
jokes.punchline: Redundantly specifying that pine trees are coniferous (which they are by definition)
jokes.explanation: Mild redundancy joke. 'Splendiferous' is a nice made-up word but the coniferous bit is weak.

jokes.text: You know, Santa may have killed Scruffy, but he makes a good point
jokes.setup: Acknowledging Santa's advice about needing a pine tree
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions Santa killed Scruffy as if it's a minor detail
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery of horrific information as casual aside. Excellent economy and dark humor.

jokes.text: Cram it, Virginia! Pine trees have been extinct for over 800 years
jokes.setup: Someone suggests getting an old-fashioned pine tree
jokes.punchline: Professor's rude dismissal plus casual mention of 800-year extinction
jokes.explanation: Good character moment with Professor's trademark rudeness and casual world-building.

jokes.text: Pine trees aren't barking snakes! They won't just turn up in a salad at olive garden
jokes.setup: Someone suggests bringing pine trees back like barking snakes
jokes.punchline: Implies barking snakes casually appear in Olive Garden salads
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully absurd implication about barking snakes in chain restaurant salads. Great world-building humor.

jokes.text: But there is one hope, and, as usual, it's Norwegian
jokes.setup: Need for hope in dire situation
jokes.punchline: 'As usual, it's Norwegian' - implying Norway always saves the day
jokes.explanation: Clever stereotype subversion - Norway as the reliable global savior. Very economical.

jokes.text: Halt! Wh... what's your business pokey-pokin' about de seed vault, uh, guardian of mankind's precious botanical heritage dere?
jokes.setup: Serious security challenge at important facility
jokes.punchline: Comically thick accent making 'pokey-pokin' and awkward formal title
jokes.explanation: Standard accent comedy with some charm in the made-up phrase and over-formal title.

jokes.text: We just want to come in and rummage about a bit. / Oh. So... okay.
jokes.setup: Formal security challenge at important scientific facility
jokes.punchline: Guard immediately accepts vague, casual explanation
jokes.explanation: Perfect anticlimax. Great economy and timing with the guard's complete capitulation.

jokes.text: Yo, Abba, what's that next-door? / Oh, dat's de germ warfare repository. / It's so close. / Is there any chance of cross-contamination? / No.
jokes.setup: Casual inquiry about neighboring facility, concern about contamination
jokes.punchline: Flat 'No' despite obviously dangerous proximity
jokes.explanation: Good setup for later contamination plot. The guard's certainty despite obvious danger is amusing.

jokes.text: Now, your pine trees were all chopped down to make emergency toilet paper during the 50-year squirts
jokes.setup: Explaining why pine trees went extinct
jokes.punchline: 'The 50-year squirts' as a historical disaster requiring all trees for toilet paper
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd historical event name. '50-year squirts' is incredibly memorable and perfectly Futurama.

jokes.text: What's that splork on them? / It's not germs, is it? / No.
jokes.setup: Obvious contamination visible on seeds, direct question about germs
jokes.punchline: Another flat 'No' despite obvious evidence
jokes.explanation: Callback to earlier denial pattern, but weaker than the original. Sets up contamination plot.

jokes.text: Could the seed have been contaminated by a virus from the germ vault? / Uh, yeah, I suppose.
jokes.setup: Obvious cause-and-effect question about contamination
jokes.punchline: Professor's casual, unconcerned confirmation of disaster
jokes.explanation: Professor's trademark scientific detachment about catastrophe. Functional but not exceptional.

jokes.text: Wait! This could be a good thing. That weaponized virus made the sickly little tree grow big and strong!
jokes.setup: Contamination causing rapid, uncontrolled growth
jokes.punchline: Professor's optimistic spin on obvious biological disaster
jokes.explanation: Sets up running gag of 'this could be a good thing' but this first instance is just setup.

jokes.text: Wait... this could be a good thing. Reforestation has begun.
jokes.setup: Trees spreading uncontrollably across the planet
jokes.punchline: Second 'this could be a good thing' as disaster escalates
jokes.explanation: Running gag building momentum. Pattern recognition starting to pay off.

jokes.text: Arguably, this could be a good thing. The planet has returned to its primeval state! Earth is just the way it was before the white man came!
jokes.setup: Third escalation of the disaster, now covering whole planet
jokes.punchline: 'Arguably' qualifier plus politically charged historical reference
jokes.explanation: Running gag peaks with most absurd rationalization and edgy historical reference.

jokes.text: Good news! All these pine trees are fighting global warming by producing oxygen! / Happy now, Gore? / Yeah. But I'd be happier if I had a set of lungs.
jokes.setup: Environmental benefit being celebrated, Gore asked if he's happy
jokes.punchline: Gore reveals he lacks lungs to benefit from oxygen
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark punchline. Great character integration with Gore's environmental activism vs. his physical state.

jokes.text: Hey, cool. The air's on fire.
jokes.setup: Catastrophic atmospheric ignition
jokes.punchline: Fry's casual, impressed reaction to apocalyptic event
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry reaction - dim but oddly impressed by disaster. Excellent economy and character voice.

jokes.text: Ho, ho, ho! Everyone's dead!
jokes.setup: Traditional jolly Santa laugh
jokes.punchline: Gleeful announcement of universal death
jokes.explanation: Perfect dark comedy. Maximum economy with traditional Santa joy applied to mass death. Instantly quotable and perfectly character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Pfft! That's just a fake holiday you make up every year to get out of work. / Yeah! If it's real, how come there's no song that explains how to celebrate it?
jokes.setup: Accusation that Robanukah is fake
jokes.punchline: Demand for explanatory song sets up musical number
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the song. Meta-joke about holiday special musical conventions.

jokes.text: Robanukah may sound as if it's Jewish / but its ancient-sounding customs are exceptionally new-ish
jokes.setup: Acknowledging the obvious Hanukkah parallel
jokes.punchline: Rhyming 'Jewish/new-ish' while admitting it's all made up
jokes.explanation: Clever rhyme scheme and self-aware about the holiday's fake nature. Good song opening.

jokes.text: So take a hearty swallow from your robo-kiddush cup / which will give me time to quickly make them up
jokes.setup: Traditional religious ceremony being described
jokes.punchline: Admits he's making up the traditions as he goes
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender move - openly admitting his scam while continuing it. Meta-joke works well.

jokes.text: Do you spin a dreidel made from Clay? / Mine is called a 'droidl', and it's rigged to make you pay
jokes.setup: Traditional dreidel question
jokes.punchline: 'Droidl' pun plus admission it's a gambling scam
jokes.explanation: Great pun and perfect Bender character integration - turning religious tradition into gambling scam.

jokes.text: Do you eat these yummy tin-wrapped chocolate coins? / Better! We got fembots with illegal five-speed groins!
jokes.setup: Traditional chocolate gelt
jokes.punchline: Escalates to explicit sexual content with technical specifications
jokes.explanation: Shocking escalation from innocent tradition to explicit content. 'Five-speed groins' is memorably crude and technical.

jokes.text: Shalom, Ruth and Esther! Why with the music so loud? Would it kill them to turn up the heat a little in here?
jokes.setup: Fembots appearing for oil wrestling
jokes.punchline: Classic Jewish grandmother complaints despite being robots
jokes.explanation: Standard stereotype humor. Competent but predictable Jewish grandmother character voices.

jokes.text: But by far the most important thing is oil / To keep the lamplight burning or to help the latkes broil? / No, we pour the holy lubricant out from the sacred vessel / into this blessed pit so they can wrestle
jokes.setup: Traditional Hanukkah oil significance being explained
jokes.punchline: Subverts to lubrication for wrestling instead of religious purposes
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection from sacred to profane. Well-structured buildup and subversion.

jokes.text: Thank you, Abraham. / Not in the hair, please. I just had it did.
jokes.setup: Ceremonial oil being poured
jokes.punchline: Fembot's vanity about her hairstyle during wrestling prep
jokes.explanation: Mild vanity joke with grammar error ('had it did'). Functional but unremarkable.

jokes.text: Aw, no! These broads are supposed to wrestle for six and half weeks, but there's only enough petroleum oil for four and a half weeks!
jokes.setup: Oil shortage discovered
jokes.punchline: Six and a half weeks of wrestling is treated as reasonable requirement
jokes.explanation: Sets up the oil shortage plot. The timeframe is absurd but joke is functional setup.

jokes.text: We're still getting paid, right? / I told you, it's an audition!
jokes.setup: Fembot asking about payment for wrestling
jokes.punchline: Bender reveals it's an unpaid audition, classic exploitation
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Bender's exploitation tactics. Economical and true to character.

jokes.text: Not kosher! It has to be petroleum oil! Do you not give a damn about the hallowed traditions of Robanukah?!
jokes.setup: Alternative oils being offered
jokes.punchline: Bender's indignant defense of 'traditions' he just made up
jokes.explanation: Good irony with Bender defending fake traditions with religious fervor. Perfect character consistency.

jokes.text: That'll be... I don't know, ten bucks? / Um, can you make change for a Nobel prize?
jokes.setup: Casual pricing for infinite solar power
jokes.punchline: Gore treating Nobel Prize as currency
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist economics and perfect Gore character moment treating his Nobel Prize casually.

jokes.text: I can't wait that long! I've got A.D.D.!
jokes.setup: Professor explaining petroleum takes millions of years to form
jokes.punchline: Bender claims A.D.D. as reason he can't wait geological timeframes
jokes.explanation: Good character logic applying human condition to geological time. Economical and fits Bender's personality.

jokes.text: This is so unfair! I don't wrestle dry, Bender. I went to Vassar.
jokes.setup: Oil shortage affecting wrestling plans
jokes.punchline: Elite education as reason for refusing to wrestle without oil
jokes.explanation: Good class-consciousness humor. Vassar education as qualification for oil wrestling standards is absurd but logical.

jokes.text: It's an albino humping worm! / Why do they call it that? / Because it doesn't have any pigment.
jokes.setup: Scary name 'albino humping worm' with natural follow-up question
jokes.punchline: Answer addresses only the innocent 'albino' part, ignoring 'humping'
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection. Perfect deadpan response that ignores the obvious question. Very economical and memorable.

jokes.text: I hate to complain about the heat, but the air conditioner's on fire.
jokes.setup: Extreme heat conditions from deep drilling
jokes.punchline: Understatement about fire being presented as minor inconvenience
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery of catastrophic situation as minor complaint. Excellent economy.

jokes.text: You vile racist. Haven't my people suffered enough?
jokes.setup: Leela's anger at risking lives for fake holiday
jokes.punchline: Bender claims racism and historical suffering for his made-up robot religion
jokes.explanation: Good irony with Bender appropriating real historical suffering for his scam. Well-executed character moment.

jokes.text: I thought they were selfish, yet in the end, it turns out it was I who thought they were selfish.
jokes.setup: Bender reflecting on his friends' death
jokes.punchline: Completely circular, meaningless statement that reveals no self-awareness
jokes.explanation: Brilliant pseudo-profound statement that means nothing. Perfect Bender fake introspection that reveals complete lack of growth.

jokes.text: There was only enough petroleum for four and a half weeks of oil wrestling, but it lasted 500 million years! It's a miracle!
jokes.setup: Traditional Hanukkah miracle of oil lasting longer than expected
jokes.punchline: Applies miracle logic to petroleum oil formed from his dead friends over geological time
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark parody of Hanukkah miracle using his friends' death as the source. Excellent structure and payoff.

jokes.text: We brought a chocolate cake, but now I'm worried that might be offensive in some way.
jokes.setup: Bringing cake to Kwanzaa celebration
jokes.punchline: Self-conscious worry about unspecified potential offense
jokes.explanation: Mild social anxiety humor about cultural sensitivity. Functional but unremarkable.

jokes.text: Well, look at the cat the cat dragged in!
jokes.setup: Traditional phrase 'look what the cat dragged in'
jokes.punchline: Says 'the cat the cat dragged in' - redundant phrasing
jokes.explanation: Weak malapropism. The redundancy isn't particularly clever or character-specific.

jokes.text: All night long, woman.
jokes.setup: LaBarbara saying she hopes he's ready to celebrate
jokes.punchline: Sexual innuendo delivered with characteristic smoothness
jokes.explanation: Standard innuendo but delivered in character. Economical but predictable.

jokes.text: Kwanzaa traditions are quite ancient, dating back over one thousand years. / Whoa.
jokes.setup: Hermes claiming Kwanzaa is ancient
jokes.punchline: Fry's impressed reaction to obviously false claim (Kwanzaa was created in 1966)
jokes.explanation: Good irony about Kwanzaa's actual recent origin. Fry's gullibility works well.

jokes.text: No, child, that's not a made-up character, it's Kwanzaabot!
jokes.setup: Someone thinking Kwanzaabot is the Kool-Aid man
jokes.punchline: Insisting he's NOT a made-up character while he obviously is
jokes.explanation: Good meta-joke about fictional characters within fiction. Self-aware humor works reasonably well.

jokes.text: How 'bout I just lay down?
jokes.setup: Asked to lay down a beat for the song
jokes.punchline: Suggests literally lying down instead
jokes.explanation: Classic Zoidberg misunderstanding with literal interpretation. Very economical and character-appropriate.

jokes.text: So sit your asses down and have some knowledge dropped upons ya
jokes.setup: Educational song about Kwanzaa principles
jokes.punchline: Street slang mixed with grammar error ('upons ya')
jokes.explanation: Standard street dialect humor with mild grammar play. Character-appropriate but unremarkable.

jokes.text: like the ever-changing nature of my sexual proclivities
jokes.setup: Explaining 'creativity' as a Kwanzaa principle
jokes.punchline: Applies it to his changing sexual preferences
jokes.explanation: Unexpected sexual content in educational context. Character-appropriate transgression.

jokes.text: something about a pine tree and an oil-wrestling dealie? / That's from Xmas and Robanukah you plagiarizing lout.
jokes.setup: Kwanzaabot mixing up holiday traditions
jokes.punchline: Bender correctly identifying cross-contamination from earlier segments
jokes.explanation: Good meta-callback acknowledging the anthology structure. Self-aware about segment blending.

jokes.text: Yeah, I'm kinda losing interest here / I best be rollin' out
jokes.setup: Educational song about Kwanzaa
jokes.punchline: Character admits he's bored with his own performance
jokes.explanation: Good meta-humor about performer losing interest in their own act. Character breaking commitment.

jokes.text: but they best be made of beeswax / or y'all might as well be white
jokes.setup: Requirement for specific type of candles
jokes.punchline: Extreme racial consequence for wrong candle material
jokes.explanation: Bold racial humor with extreme consequences for minor detail. Very economical and memorable.

jokes.text: Girl, those stank sticks are for single women who take baths!
jokes.setup: Scented candles being suggested as substitute
jokes.punchline: Specific demographic insult about scented candle users
jokes.explanation: Good character voice with specific demographic targeting. 'Stank sticks' is memorable phrasing.

jokes.text: Sweet candelabra of la habra, labarbara!
jokes.setup: Hermes expressing frustration
jokes.punchline: Made-up exclamation combining candelabra, La Habra (California city), and his wife's name
jokes.explanation: Creative made-up expletive with good wordplay connecting multiple elements. Very character-specific.

jokes.text: The bees are acting all crazy, like they've been drinking tang and cough syrup, or, as I call it, my Friday night.
jokes.setup: Describing erratic bee behavior
jokes.punchline: Reveals she drinks the same substance combination recreationally
jokes.explanation: Good self-deprecating humor revealing character's substance use. Tang and cough syrup is memorably specific.

jokes.text: Die young, leave a pretty corpse, that's what I say. / You should say something else.
jokes.setup: Bender's philosophy about dying young
jokes.punchline: Leela's direct criticism of his life philosophy
jokes.explanation: Perfect straight-man response to Bender's inappropriate philosophy. Excellent economy and timing.

jokes.text: Okay. If we survive.
jokes.setup: Being asked to say something in bee talk
jokes.punchline: Casual acknowledgment they'll probably die
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan acknowledgment of danger. Very economical but not particularly memorable.

jokes.text: It's like the deadly prius.
jokes.setup: Commenting on how quiet it is
jokes.punchline: Compares silence to hybrid car's quiet operation as if it's sinister
jokes.explanation: Clever observation about Prius being dangerously quiet. Very economical and specific cultural reference.

jokes.text: That was weird. It's like the Jagermeister air show all over again.
jokes.setup: Bees flying in strange formation
jokes.punchline: Implies there was a Jagermeister-sponsored air show with similar bizarre results
jokes.explanation: Good absurd world-building reference. Jagermeister air show implies drunken aviation disasters.

jokes.text: The big, fat, ugly, compound-eyed, hairy-thorax... / Don't be applying your euro-centric standards of beauty to me, fool.
jokes.setup: Fry describing queen bee with human beauty standards
jokes.punchline: Queen bee calling out cultural imperialism in beauty standards
jokes.explanation: Brilliant social commentary via talking bee. 'Euro-centric beauty standards' from a space bee is perfect absurdist wisdom.

jokes.text: Are y'all black with yellow stripes, or ya yellow with black stripes? / Yellow with black stripes, man. / Why don't you just move along?
jokes.setup: Bees arguing about their color pattern
jokes.punchline: Dismissive response to correct answer about their own appearance
jokes.explanation: Mild absurdist humor about bees not knowing their own coloring. Functional but unremarkable.

jokes.text: Let me ask you something. You sting my wife? / What kinda buzzin' question is that? / Where do you get the wings big enough to ask me that?
jokes.setup: Confrontational questions between bees
jokes.punchline: Bee-specific insults using 'buzzin' and wing size as measure of audacity
jokes.explanation: Good species-specific adaptation of confrontational dialogue. 'Wings big enough' is a creative insult.

jokes.text: It's time to African-americanize these honeybees!
jokes.setup: Hermes deciding to intervene in bee conflict
jokes.punchline: Made-up verb for applying African-American unity principles to space bees
jokes.explanation: Creative verb formation and character-appropriate cultural bridge-building. Memorable phrasing.

jokes.text: At the end of the day, we're just a couple dern talkin' space bees.
jokes.setup: Reconciliation between fighting bees
jokes.punchline: Reduces the absurd situation to its basic elements with folksy phrasing
jokes.explanation: Perfect meta-acknowledgment of the absurd premise with down-to-earth wisdom. Very quotable.

jokes.text: Hooray! Kwanzaabot's here to save... Oh, they killed him.
jokes.setup: Bender's enthusiasm about Kwanzaabot arriving
jokes.punchline: Immediate deflation as he realizes Kwanzaabot is dead
jokes.explanation: Perfect anticlimax structure. Excellent economy in showing hope immediately crushed.

jokes.text: featuring Bender, Leela, and me, Al Gore, as Captain Lance Starman
jokes.setup: Promotional announcement for future episodes
jokes.punchline: Al Gore randomly inserted as space captain character
jokes.explanation: Absurd casting choice - Al Gore as space captain is unexpected and specific enough to be funny

tmdb_overview: The Planet Express crew learns the true meaning of Xmas, Robanukah and Kwanzaa.
